titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 615

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=54 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-06 15:19:34.009024+00 | translation moa=53 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-06 15:19:34.021513+00

div.p:
. Alexander . dei Gratia Rex Scottorum : Episcopis . Abbatibus . Prioribus . Comitibus . Iustic’ . Baronibus . Vicecomitibus . Prepositis . Ministris . ⁊ Omnibus probis hominibus
Tocius terre sue . Clericis ⁊ laicis  Salutem . Sciatis . quod uolo ⁊ firmiter precipio . quod nemora monachorum de Coldingham . vide
licet . Grenewde .⁊ totum nemus de Ristuna . ⁊ Broccheholewde . ⁊ Akesside . ⁊ Chirchedeneswde . ⁊ Harewde . ⁊ Denewde .
⁊ Swinewde . ⁊ Hundewde . ⁊ omnia nemora ⁊ Gwastina sua sint sub defensione prioris ⁊ custodia . necnon ⁊ monachorum
de Coldingham . ne aliquis super decem libras forisfacture . quicquam in predictis nemoribus uel Gwastinis capiat  nisi per ipsum pri
orem . uel per monachos prenominati loci  si ipse presens non fuerit . exceptis tantummodo necessariis de castello meo
de Berewic’ . que mihimet ipsi solummodo conueniunt . Et si clientes mei pro necessariis meis ad opus castelli mei de
Berewic’ . ad nemora predicta uenerint  per priorem uel per clientes suos . que opus fuerint mihi . et ubi ipse uel Clientes
sui monstrauerint accipiant . Insuper addo et illis concedo . quod predictus prior et monachi habeant Gwarennam in pre
dictis nemoribus . ⁊ per totam terram suam . sicut extenduntur Recte ⁊ antique diuise . uidelicet . a diuisis inter Berewic’ . ⁊
Lambertunam  usque ad Bilie . ⁊ usque ad Drieforde . ⁊ ita per Mereburne . usque ad Crachochtre . ⁊ usque ad Eieforde . ⁊
usque ad Riuulum qui manat in mare per Aldecambuspethe . ita quod nullus ibi aliquid capiat . nec lingna . nec aliquam
bestiam siluestrem  nisi per ipsos . Quod si aliquis in predictis nemoribus uel Gwastinis capiatur uenando . uel lingna secando
uel aliquid inde accipiendo . preter licentiam prioris ⁊ monachorum  habeant prior ⁊ monachi . quod super illum ⁊ cum illo
inuenerint . ⁊ ego forisfacturam prenominatam . sicut Carte Regis . DauiD . ⁊ Regis Willelmi . patris mei testantur . ⁊ Con
firmant . Testibus . Willelmo . de Boscho . Cancellario . meo . Comite . Malcolmo . de Fif : Magistro Adam . de Scon’ . ⁊ Roberto . Capellanis meis . Philippo .
de Lundin’ . DauiD . marescallo . Heruico . marescallo . Adam . Hasteng : Apud SelescheR’ . ultimo die Iulii .

Alexander, by the Grace of God King of Scots, to Bishops, Abbots, Priors, Earls, Justices, Barons, Sheriffs, Provosts, Officials, and All worthy men of the Whole of his land, Clerk and lay : Greeting. Know that I will and firmly instruct that the woods of the monks of Coldingham, namely, Greenwood, and the whole wood of Reston, and Brockholeswood, and Aikieside, and 'Chirchedeneswde', and 'Harewde', and 'Denewde', and Swinewood, and Houndwood, and all their other woods and wastelands shall be under the protection and custody of the prior as well as of the monks of Coldingham. Let no one, on pain of the forfeiture of ten pounds, take anything in the aforesaid woods or wastelands, except through the prior, or through the monks of the aforenamed place if he is not himself present, except for the requirements of my castle at Berwick alone, those things only which are proper to me myself. And if my servants shall come to the aforesaid woods for my needs for the maintenance of my castle at Berwick, by the prior or through his servants, and where he or his servants show what things are necessary for me, they shall receive them. I furthermore add and grant to them, that the aforesaid prior and monks shall have warren in the aforesaid woods, and throughought the whole of their land, as it stretches by its right and ancient boundaries, namely, from the boundaries between Berwick and Lamberton as far as Biollie and as far as 'Drieforde' and so by the 'Mereburne' as far as 'Crachoctre' and as far as 'Eieforde' and as far as the burn which flows into the sea by Oldcambus peth, so that nothing shall be taken there, neither firewood nor any woodland beast, except by themselves. If anyone shall be caught hunting in the aforesaid woods or wastelands, or cutting firewood, or taking away anything from there without the permission of the prior and monks, the prior and monks shall have that which they find upon him and with him, and I shall make the aforenamed forfeiture, as the Charters of King David and of King William my father bear witness and confirm. As witnesses : William del Bois, my Chancellor; Earl Malcolm of Fife; Master Adam of Scone and Robert, my Chaplains; Philip of Lundin; David the marischal; Hervey the marischal; Adam Hastings. At Selkirk on the last day of July.
